title: hieracium sparsum subsp. maricae
intro: Hieracium sparsum subsp. maricae, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium sparsum subsp. maricae include its potential to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
